subject: [PATCH RFC v3 6/6] selftests/mm: cover /proc/pid/mem access to VM_PFNMAP memory

2026-07-17.patch_summary: This patch adds two tests to the selftests/mm/pfnmap.c file, which cover reading VM_PFNMAP memory through /proc/pid/mem. The first test, procmem_cow_read, maps a private and writable file, writes to a copy-on-write (COW) page, and then reads it back through /proc/self/mem. This exercise the __access_remote_vm() function two ways: directly returning a struct page for COWed pages, and routing to vma->vm_ops->access() for raw PFNs. The second test, procmem_pfn_read, reads a raw Physical Frame Number (PFN) back through ->access(), which is only possible for genuine device memory. These tests ensure that the kernel's handling of VM_PFNMAP memory is correct and consistent.
